untranslatable_reason: Pattern detection requires geometric analysis of trend lines (horizontal resistance with 2+ touches, down-sloping lower trend line with 2+ touches, expanding width) which cannot be expressed with single-bar indicator comparisons. The signal requires multi-bar pattern recognition and visual megaphone shape validation that exceeds the scope of point-in-time indicator conditions.
translation_notes: This descending broadening formation is a complex multi-bar pattern requiring detection of: (1) horizontal resistance line with minimum 2 touches, (2) down-sloping support line with minimum 2 touches, (3) expanding width over time, and (4) volume dome pattern. Standard indicators cannot capture these geometric relationships. A backtest engine would need custom pattern detection logic or machine learning classification. The entry conditions use volume increase as a proxy signal, but this does not capture the actual pattern structure described by Bulkowski.
